meaning: adj.非正统的,异教的,异端的
examples: He also used ski poles in an unorthodox way—to propel himself as he skied.
The reality-based show followed the unorthodox lives of Ozzy, his wife Sharon, daughter Kelly, and son, Jack.
Most successful people are unorthodox persons whose minds wonder outside traditional ways of thinking.
It came to light the Countrywide was in serious trouble with risky unorthodox loans.
He uses an unorthodox, angular, herky-jerky delivery that relies more on dexterity than power.
